title: graphardisia bartlettii
intro: Graphardisia bartlettii, also known as the false goldenseal, is a plant species that belongs to the Annonaceae family. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The medicinal properties of Graphardisia bartlettii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Graphardisia bartlettii include dried roots and leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing.
